California
Fair Political
Practices Cornrnission
Angil Morris
Office of the City AttOrney
900 N. Palm Street
Turlock′  CA  95380
」une 20, 
■990
Re:
Your Request for lnforma■
Assistance/COnfirmation of Te■
ephone
Adv■ce
Our Fi■e No. ■-90-373
Dear Ms. Morris:
we have received your letter dated May 2L, L99o seeking
advice regarding the application of the conflict of interest
provisioni of the Politica1 Reform Act ("the a"trr11 to two menbers
-of tne TurLock City Council, Mayor Curt Andre and Council-member
carolyn Ratto. Your request fol-losrs prior informal telephone
advic6 provided to you by this agency on May 18, 1990, regarding
the ob1-igations of the two counc j-lrnembers. We provide you with
advice as the authorized representative of Mayor Andre and
councilmember Ratto, but because your request does not involve a
specific pending decision, v/e are treating it as one for informal
assistance onfy, pursuant'to Reg'ulation rclzo 1c'1 (copy enclosed) ' 2
OUESTTONS
(1) Does Mayor Andre, a practicing optometrist in the city
of tuilock, have i disqualifying conflict of interest concerning
any matter which involves one of his patients?
(2) Does council-member Ratto, whose spouse is a practi'cing
aentist'in the city of Turlock, have a disqualifying conflict of
i.nterest concerning any matter $/hich involves a patient of her
spouse's practice?
1 Gor"a.rr"nt Code Sections BI9OO-91015. AI1 statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated'
2 rnforma] assistance does not
immunity Provided bY an oPinion
tion ealfa; Regulation 18329 (c)
provide the requestor with the
or formal written advice. (Sec-
(3).).
428 J Street, Suite 8OO a P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 95804‐0807 0 (916)322‐ 5660


FiLe No. I-9 0-3 7 3
Page 2
CONCLUSION
(1) Mayor Andre must abstain from participating in any deci-
sion involving any of his patients if the patient has been a
source of j,ncome to hin, or has promised income to hin, of $250 or
more within 12 months prior to the tine the decision is made.
(2) councilmenber Ratto must abstain from participating in
any decision involving any of her spouse's patj.ents if the patient
has been a source of incorne to her spouse, or has promised income
to her spouse, of $5oo or more within 12 months prior to the tine
the decision is made.
FACTS
curt Andre was elected mayor of the city of Turlock in April
1990. Dr. Andre, a practicing optonetrist in the cj-ty, is the
sole shareholder of Andre, Inc., through which he conducts his
practice with another optometrist and fron which both draw a sal-
.ry. 
Each optometrist sees a group of patients, and some of the
palients maintain health insurance which, \'rhen appropriate, con-
-stitutes the payor for the services provided to the two groups of
patients. 
you have indicated that Dr. Andre is a listed provider
in a ttpreferred provider organizationrr and patients select him
from a listing of available optometrists.
Carolyn Ratto was elected to the city of Turlock city council
in April, 1990. Ms. Ratto's husband ("Dr. Ratto") is a practicing
dentist in the city, and conducts his practice as a sole practi-
tioner.
ANALYSI S
The Act prohibits a public official 
frorn making, participat-
ing in, or usj-ng his official position to influence a governmental
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a finan-
cial interest. 
(Section 87100.) A public official 
has a finan-
cial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that
the decision will have a rnaterial financial effect, distinguish-
able from its effect on the public generally, on the official, 
a
member of official's 
immediate fanily, or on any source of income
aggregating 9250 or rnore within 12 months prior to the time v/hen
the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) A public official 
al,so
has a f j-nancial interest in a decision when it is reasonably fore-
seeable that the decision will have a naterial financial impact on
any business entity in which the public official. is a director,
officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of man-
agement. (section 871o3(d).) In addition, Section 82030 pro-
vides that the income of an individual also includes any comnunj-ty
property interest in the income of a spouse.
Because Dr. Andre is the elected mayor of the City of
Turlock, he is a public official 
(section 82048), and therefore


File No. l-9o-373
Page 3
any person or business that has been a source of income to hi,n of
$250 or rnore wj.thin the past twelve nonths may constitute a
disqualj,fying economic interest as defined in Section 871-03.
Because Ms. Ratto is an el-ected councilmember of the city of
Turlock, she is a public official 
(section 82048), and therefore
any person or business that has been a source of incorne to her
spouse of $5oo or more within the past twel-ve months nay consti-
tute a disqualifying econorni.c interest as defined in Section
87103.
Foreseeable ttlaterial Financial Effect
The effect of a decision is reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that j.t wj.1l- occur. While certainty
is not required, an effect that is merely a possi.bility is not
reasonably foreseeable. 
( Do\^i nev Cares v. Do!'rnev communitv
Development com. (1987) 196 cal.App.3d 983; In re Thorner (1975)
l" FPPC ops. l-98. ) To determine whether the effect of a decision
on either Dr. Andre's or Ms' Ratto's financial interests is fore-
seeable, the facts specj-f ic to each decisj,on must be assessed' If
it is determined that the effect of a decision is foreseeable,
Dr. Andre and Ms. Ratto are disqualified from participation when
the effect of the decision wiLl- be materiaf and such effect wil-1
Ue aistinguishable fron the effect upon the public genera11y.3
(1) Dr. Andre
when a patient of Dr. Andre appears before the council on a
rnatter, Regulation la702.1(a) (1) (copy enclosed) is appl-icable.
This regul,ation provides that erhen a source of income of $250 or
more is directly before the city counciL as an applicant or the
subject of the decision, the effect of the decision j.s deerned
rnaterial and disqualification is required. 
(Densmore Advice Let-
ter, No. A-9O-22a; combs Advice Letter, No. A-89-177, copies en-
closed. ) 4
' 
Although section 87103.5 provides for a linited exception to
the conflict of interest laws for income received fron "retail
sales, rr we have not considered the provision of professional
health care services as a trretail salert for purposes of this
analysis.
4 A =o.r."" of income is directly before the city council when the
source initiates the proceeding by filing an application, cIaim,
appeal, or sirniLar request, or is otherwj,se a named party in, or
the subject of, the proceeding. A person or business entity is
the subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the issuance,
renewal, approval-, denial, or revocation of any license, perrnit,
or other entitlernent to, or contract with, the subject person or
business entity. 
(Regulation La7o2.1(b) . )


File No. l-9o-373
Page 4
You have indi,cated that Dr. Andre and his associate both draw
a sal-ary from Andre, Inc. 
Because he is the sole shareholder of
Andre, Inc., or. Andre has a financial- interest in a decision !'rhen
it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a mate-
rial financial irnpact on Andre, Inc- (section 87103(d).) When
clients of Dr. Andre appear before the city council, Andre, Inc.
is not directly before the agency and Regulation 18702.2 (copy
enclosed) applies. This regulation provides that for a relatively
srnall busineis entity, the indirect effect of a decision is rnate-
rial where:
(1) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease in the gross revenues for a fiscal year
of $10,000 or more; or
(2) The decision will- result in the business
entity incurring or avoiding additional expenses or
reducing or eliminating existing expenses for a
fiscal year in the amount ot 52,500 or nore; or
(3) The decision will result in the increase
or decrease in the value of assets or liabilities
of $10,000 or more.
Regulation \87 02.2 (g) .
A client of Andre, Inc. is considered a source of income to
Dr. Andre under section 82030, and must therefore be considered in
analyzinq his duties under the Act. 
(Leach Advice Letter, No'
I-90--181; webb Advice Letter, No. I-89-415, copies enclosed') If
a patient of Or. Andre I^Iho is not a source of income of $250 or
rnoie to Dr, Andre aPpears before the council for a decisi'on, Dr'
Andre will Ue reguiiea to disqualify hirnself fron participating in
the decision ie €he decision will have the above-noted impact on
Andre, Inc. 
(see zaLtzman Advice Letter, No. A-90-293, copy en-
cJ.osed . )
Because Dr. Andre is the sole sharehol-der in Andre, Inc',
the potential conf 1j-ct presented by tl9 aPpearance of one of
Dr.-indre's patients before the council vJould not be obviated
sinply Uy or. Andre transferring all of his patients to his col-
feaiue; Lhe patient would sti1l rernain a client of the business
under Section azO:0, and Dr. Andre still 
remains the sole
shareholder of Andre, Inc. 
(webb Advice Letter, supra. )
The sources tapped by Dr. Andre's patients in order to pay
for the professional services received does not change this con-
clusion. 
You have indicated that because sone of Dr. Andre's pa-
tients are covered by insurance policies, the j-nsurance company
rrbecomes the payor'r to Dr. Andre for the services provided to the
patient. 
Nevertheless, the patient exercises sufficient control
over the income received by Dr. Andre, sinply by selecting Dr.
Andre instead of other health care providers for the necessary

Page 5
care, so as
vice Letter to constitute a source of income to him.
No. I-89-555, coPY enclosed- )
(Larsen Ad―
(2) Ms. Ratto
when a patient of Dr. Ratto comes before the councj-I,
Ms. Ratto does not receive income directly from the patient-
Hov/ever, because Section 82030 defines rr incomerr to incl-ude the
conrnunity property interest in the incone of a spouse, a patient
of Dr. natto is a source of incorne to Ms- Ratto; consequently she
may not participate in any decision which will have a reasonably
foieseeatl-e rnaterj-a1 financial effect on her spouse's source(s) of
income .
when a patient of Dr. Ratto appears before the council on a
matter, Regulation La7o2.1(a) (1) provides that the effect of the
decision with respect to Ms. Ratto is deemed material and
disqualif ication is required-

cornfs Advice Letter, supra.) Because the disqualifying income is
lrer c-ornnunity property interest in her spouse's income, .the
requisite thie;ho1d gould be $5oo or more in incorne during the
prdceding 12 months.5
Sinilarly, the sources tapped by the patients of Dr. Ratto in
order to pay ior the professional servj-ces they receive does not
change this- conclusion. You have indicated that a large self-
insuied ernployer in the city, Foster Farms, has employees $/ho are
patients oi or. Ratto and that Foster Farms had or wj'11- have pro-
jects that come before the city council. 
Because the patient ex-
6rcises sufficient control' over the incone received by Dr. Ratto,
sirnply by selecting hin instead of other health care providers for
the- necessary care, it is the patient, and not Foster Farms, that
constitutes a source of income to hin, and -- therefore -- to
Ms. Ratto as we1L. (Larsen Advice Letter, suDra-)
Public GeDelaIlY ExcePtion
Shou1d it be deterrnined that Dr. Andre or Ms. Ratto have a
financial interest/source of incone that will be financially af-
fected by the decision, the tlro still- nay participate if the ef-
fect on Lheir i.nterests is not distinquishable from the effect on
the public generally. 
(Regulation La703, copy enclosed.) The
'tpublictr consi.sts of the entire jurisdiction of the agency in
question (In re Owen (1976) 2 FPPC Ops. 77, copY enclosed) -- in
this instance, the city of Turlock. This is so because al-f of the
residents in the jurisdiction are Dr. Andre's and Ms. Ratto's con-
stituents. 
(In re Leqan (1985) 9 FPPC Ops. 1, copy enclosed. )

CONCLUSION: (1) Mayor Andre must abstain from participating in any deci-
sion involving any of his patients if the patient has been a
source of j,ncome to hin, or has promised income to hin, of $250 or
more within 12 months prior to the tine the decision is made.
(2) councilmenber Ratto must abstain from participating in
any decision involving any of her spouse's patj.ents if the patient
